title: doratoxylon apetalum var. diphyllum
intro: Doratoxylon apetalum var. diphyllum, also known as the Diphyllum or two-leaved Doratoxylon, is a plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae. It is a medicinal plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, where it is valued for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The health benefits of Doratoxylon apetalum var. diphyllum include reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating various inflammatory conditions. The plant is often prepared as a decoction, made by boiling its leaves and roots in water, and is also used in herbal remedies such as ointments and poultices.
